notes: Triggering is the dominant failure mode. should_trigger MUST fire; should_not_trigger are near-misses that must NOT pull this in (CLI/Python image conversion, the textmode.js library, or generic non-React rendering); evals are authoring tasks whose output is verifiable React/TS code.
should_trigger: Add an image-to-ASCII effect to my React site using ascii-img-react.
How do I theme the <AsciiImage> output green-on-black with CSS variables?
Make a clicky ripple ASCII animation over a hero image on my landing page.
My <AsciiImage> is blurry/low-detail — which props sharpen the edges?
Use the ascii-img-react low-level utilities to build a custom character lookup.
Drop an ASCII portrait into my Astro page, served same-origin for CORS.
Add a rain animation to my ASCII image with ascii-img-react.
should_not_trigger: Make ASCII art of this cat and save it as a text file.
Convert this image to ASCII in Python with Pillow.
Build a real-time textmode.js webcam-to-ASCII sketch.
Render text to a 2D canvas with the canvas API.
Turn this logo PNG into an ASCII .txt for my README from the command line.

evals.prompt: Add <AsciiImage> to a React component rendering /portrait.jpg at 100 columns, green on black, ripple off.
evals.expected_output: Imports { AsciiImage } from 'ascii-img-react'; sets src, width={100}, color (green), backgroundColor (black), enableRipple={false}; notes the image must be same-origin or CORS-accessible.

evals.prompt: Theme all ASCII instances site-wide with a Fira Code font and a dark palette via CSS.
evals.expected_output: Sets --ascii-font-family / --ascii-color / --ascii-background on a shared class, passes it via className, and uses a prefers-color-scheme block for light mode.

evals.prompt: The ASCII render's internal edges look mushy. Tune it.
evals.expected_output: Raises contrast and/or directionalContrast (keeping enableDirectionalContrast true), explains the global-vs-directional contrast distinction, and warns about the >150-column animation performance cliff.

evals.prompt: Use ascii-img-react in my Next.js App Router hero.
evals.expected_output: Marks the component "use client" (it uses useEffect + a canvas), renders <AsciiImage src> from a same-origin /public asset, and sets width in characters.
